**China North Industries Group Corporation Limited**,[[3]](#cite_note-3) doing business internationally as **Norinco Group** (an abbreviation of "North Industries Corporation"), and known within China as **China Ordnance Industries Group Corporation Limited** ([Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 中国兵器工业集团有限公司[[4]](#cite_note-4)), is a Chinese [state-owned](/wiki/State-owned "State-owned") [defense corporation](/wiki/Defense_contractor "Defense contractor") that manufactures commercial and military products. Norinco Group is one of the world's largest defense contractors.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

The company's subsidiary, **China North Industries Corporation** ([Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): 中国北方工业有限公司[[7]](#cite_note-7)), or simply **Norinco**,[[8]](#cite_note-8) markets Norinco Group's products internationally, and is also involved in domestic [civil construction](/wiki/Civil_engineering "Civil engineering") and military defense projects.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Some of Norinco's international customers include [Iran](/wiki/Iran "Iran"), [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan"), [Zimbabwe](/wiki/Zimbabwe "Zimbabwe"), [Saudi Arabia](/wiki/Saudi_Arabia "Saudi Arabia") and the [Democratic Republic of the Congo](/wiki/Democratic_Republic_of_the_Congo "Democratic Republic of the Congo"), where it negotiated arms-for-minerals deals, as well as [Venezuela](/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela").[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norinco&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Established in 1980 with the approval of the [State Council of the People's Republic of China](/wiki/State_Council_of_the_People%27s_Republic_of_China "State Council of the People's Republic of China"), Norinco is an enterprise group engaged in both products and capital operation, integrated with research and development, manufacturing, marketing and services. Norinco mainly deals with defense products, petroleum and mineral resources development, international engineering contracting, optronic products, civilian explosives and chemical products, sporting arms and equipment, vehicles and logistics operation.

### U.S. sanctions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norinco&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: U.S. sanctions")]

Further information: [United States sanctions against China](/wiki/United_States_sanctions_against_China "United States sanctions against China")

In 1993, the import of most Norinco firearms and ammunition into the United States was blocked under new trade rules when China's [permanent normal trade relations](/wiki/Permanent_normal_trade_relations "Permanent normal trade relations") status was renewed. The prohibition did not apply to sporting shotguns or shotgun [ammunition](/wiki/Ammunition "Ammunition"), however. In 1994, [U.S. Customs](/wiki/U.S._Customs "U.S. Customs") agents conducted a [sting operation](/wiki/Sting_operation "Sting operation") named Operation Dragon Fire against [Atlanta](/wiki/Atlanta "Atlanta")-based importers of Norinco firearms as well as [Poly Technologies](/wiki/Poly_Technologies "Poly Technologies"). Seven officials were arrested after agreeing to smuggle 2,000 [fully automatic](/wiki/Fully_automatic "Fully automatic") [Chinese-made variants of AK-47s](/wiki/Type_56_assault_rifle "Type 56 assault rifle") to undercover agents the officials believed may have been connected to the [American Mafia](/wiki/American_Mafia "American Mafia"). At least one official, Hammond Ku, attempted to sell Chinese-produced tanks and rocket launchers to the undercover agents.[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

In August 2003, the [Bush administration](/wiki/Presidency_of_George_W._Bush "Presidency of George W. Bush") imposed sanctions on Norinco for allegedly selling missile-related goods to [Iran](/wiki/Iran "Iran").[[14]](#cite_note-14) These sanctions led to a prohibition on imports into the US of the remaining types of firearms and ammunition not covered by the 1993 ban.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

In November 2020, [Donald Trump](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump") issued an [executive order](/wiki/Executive_Order_13959 "Executive Order 13959") prohibiting any American company or individual from owning shares in companies that the [United States Department of Defense](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_Defense "United States Department of Defense") has listed as having links to the [People's Liberation Army](/wiki/People%27s_Liberation_Army "People's Liberation Army"), which included Norinco Group.[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18) In June 2021, [Joe Biden](/wiki/Joe_Biden "Joe Biden") issued [Executive Order 14032](/wiki/Executive_Order_14032 "Executive Order 14032") expanding the scope of the national emergency declared in order 13959.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

### Russian invasion of Ukraine

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norinco&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Russian invasion of Ukraine")]

Further information: [China and the Russian invasion of Ukraine](/wiki/China_and_the_Russian_invasion_of_Ukraine "China and the Russian invasion of Ukraine")

In March 2023, *[Politico](/wiki/Politico "Politico")* reported that Norinco shipped assault rifles, drone parts, and body armor to Russia between June and December 2022.[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21)

### Gaza war

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Norinco&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Gaza war")]

Further information: [Gaza war](/wiki/Gaza_war "Gaza war")

Media reports indicate that Hamas have used thousands of Norinco-made assault rifles in the war.[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23)[[24]](#cite_note-24)
